title: Confirm and Cancel Icons for Category and Line of Business Actions
story: As a DataTools Pro user editing categories or lines of business, I want clear confirm and cancel icons, so that I understand my options without guessing what the red X on the edit icon means.
description:
Replace the confusing edit+red-X icon pattern with distinct confirm (check) and cancel (close) icons for category and line of business dropdown actions.

## Icons
- `bi bi-check-square` — confirm action
- `bi bi-x-square` — cancel action

Both icons perform the same technical function (close the dropdown), but the two-icon pattern is more intuitive for users. Usability testing showed users interpreted the red X as "delete" rather than "close/dismiss".

## Context
Changes are auto-saved when the user selects a value, so both confirm and cancel effectively just close the dropdown. The dual-icon pattern provides psychological confirmation that the action was completed.
acceptance_criteria: Check and close icons appear as a pair for category/LOB dropdown actions
Icons use bi-check-square and bi-x-square from Bootstrap Icons
Icons are right-aligned and properly sized
Previous edit+red-X icon pattern is replaced
ux_changes: Category and LOB dropdown actions now show paired confirm/cancel icons (check + close) instead of the confusing edit+red-X pattern. Driven by usability testing feedback.
implementation_notes: Usability testing revealed users interpreted the red X as a delete action rather than a dismiss action. The check+close pair provides clearer intent even though both perform the same function (close the dropdown).
comment_summary: Developer initially asked for clarification on the check icon's purpose. Ryan explained the usability testing feedback — users confused the red X with delete. The dual icon pattern provides clear confirm/cancel semantics even though both close the dropdown.
